98.reason: [Error: When nextToken() returns null (end of input), calling token.getType() will cause a NullPointerException. The loop condition checks isEndOfInput() before calling nextToken(), but nextToken() may return null even when not at end of input (e.g., if no pattern matches). Additionally, the code does not handle the case where token is null, leading to a crash.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When nextToken() returns null (end of input), calling token.getType() will cause a NullPointerException. The loop condition checks isEndOfInput() before calling nextToken(), but nextToken() may return null even when not at end of input (e.g., if no pattern matches). Additionally, the code does not handle the case where token is null, leading to a crash.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When nextToken() returns null (end of input), calling token.getType() will cause a NullPointerException. The loop condition !isEndOfInput() may not prevent this because nextToken() can return null even when not at end of input if no pattern matches, or the end-of-input check is inconsistent.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Potential NullPointerException if nextToken() returns null for unmatched input or EOF.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Missing null check for `token` returned by `nextToken()`, which will cause NullPointerException when encountering unrecognized characters or at EOF.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [This line will crash with NullPointerException if token is null. Additionally, the logic writes both correct tokens and error tokens to sb_output, but specification says: for erroneous source programs, only error info should be output (and only to error.txt). However, the problem states each test has exactly one lexical error, so upon detecting any error, the program should stop normal token output and only report the error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If nextToken() returns null (e.g., due to unrecognized lexeme), calling token.getType() throws NullPointerException]

101.reason: [Error: If token is null, token.toString() will throw NullPointerException. The code should check for null before using token.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: If token is null, token.toString() will throw NullPointerException. The code should check for null before using token.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: If token is null, token.toString() will throw NullPointerException. The code does not handle the case where nextToken() returns null.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [This writes every token—including error tokens—to lexer.txt, which violates the spec: if there's any lexical error, output should go only to error.txt, not lexer.txt. Also, if token is null, this throws NullPointerException.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Similarly, token.toString() will throw NullPointerException if token is null]

140.reason: This causes program to crash if input file does not exist, leading to empty output.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  This causes program to crash if input file does not exist, leading to empty output.

172.reason: Error: lineNum is zero-indexed (starts at 0), but the expected output requires line numbers starting from 1. This will cause all error line numbers to be off by one.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: lineNum is zero-indexed (starts at 0), but the expected output requires line numbers starting from 1. This will cause all error line numbers to be off by one.

118.reason: Returns 0-based index, but problem requires line numbers starting from 1.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Returns 0-based index, but problem requires line numbers starting from 1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Returns 0-based index, but the problem specification requires error line numbers to start from 1.

178.reason: Advances pointer past the entire contiguous non-whitespace sequence, but nextToken() only matches a prefix, causing subsequent characters to be skipped/lost.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Advances pointer past the entire contiguous non-whitespace sequence, but nextToken() only matches a prefix, causing subsequent characters to be skipped/lost.

78.reason: Consumes the entire word and advances the file pointer, but only a prefix is matched as a token, leaving the rest of the word unprocessed and lost.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Consumes the entire word and advances the file pointer, but only a prefix is matched as a token, leaving the rest of the word unprocessed and lost.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Consumes the entire contiguous non-whitespace sequence, but the source pointer is not rolled back for the unmatched suffix, causing subsequent tokens to be skipped or misaligned.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [This reads an entire non-whitespace sequence as a "word", but lexical analysis must tokenize incrementally character-by-character or by longest match; reading whole words prevents correct recognition of adjacent tokens like "&&" vs "& &" and causes incorrect matching against patterns that expect only a prefix.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Returns 0-based index, but problem requires 1-based line numbers for error output.

89.reason: [If no token matches, it returns null, but the caller does not handle null tokens, leading to NullPointerException when accessing token.getType()]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [If no pattern matches the entire currentWord (or its prefix), this returns null, but the program then tries to call token.getType() in TokenLize(), causing a NullPointerException. Moreover, it fails to detect lexical errors like standalone '&' because the word may be longer than the error pattern (e.g., "&x" is read as one word, so "&" alone isn't matched).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If no token matches, it returns null, but the caller does not handle null tokens, leading to NullPointerException when accessing token.getType()]

96.reason: [If nextToken() returns null (e.g., on unrecognized input), the following lines will throw a NullPointerException when calling token.getType(). Also, even if an ERROR_AND or ERROR_OR token is matched, the code still appends to sb_output, violating the requirement that erroneous programs should only output to error.txt.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [If nextToken() returns null (e.g., on unrecognized input), the following lines will throw a NullPointerException when calling token.getType().
